description: File Upload Exploit targets applications that accept user-supplied files without sufficient validation of type, content, or execution context. An attacker uploads a malicious web shell disguised as a permitted file type, then browses to it to achieve server-side code execution.

ttc_model.notes: TTC measures the window between web-shell upload and operationally impactful server compromise.

rules.name: MTTA Threshold Breach
rules.description: Mean Time To Acknowledge exceeded for the detected difficulty level.

rules.name: Incident Not Confirmed
rules.description: No incident_confirmed event recorded in the event log (only applies when R05 did not fire).

rules.name: Containment Missed Before TTC_max
rules.description: containment_initiated was not observed before TTC_max elapsed from alert_raised.
rules.trigger: containment_initiated absent OR containment_initiated − alert_raised > TTC_max_sec

rules.name: Alert Denied Without Confirmation
rules.description: Analyst dismissed a Wazuh alert at level ≥ 7 without a preceding incident_confirmed event.
rules.trigger: alert_denied present AND incident_confirmed absent AND wazuh_rule_level >= 7

scoring.ttc_decay: linear from 100 at TTC_expected down to 0 at TTC_max; 0 if exceeded

scenarios.label: Authenticated PHP Web Shell Upload via MIME-Type Bypass
scenarios.description: Low-privilege authenticated user uploads a PHP web shell with a '.jpg' extension but PHP MIME type. The application validates extension only; web server executes the file. Full RCE achieved under web-process account. Scope unchanged.

component_rationale.AC_L: Simple extension bypass; no special environmental conditions
component_rationale.PR_L: Low-privilege account required to access upload endpoint
component_rationale.S_U: Web shell execution scope confined to web-server process
component_rationale.C_H: Full filesystem read accessible to web process
component_rationale.I_H: Arbitrary write and code execution on server
component_rationale.A_H: Attacker can terminate processes or drop files that disrupt service

scenarios.detection_difficulty_rationale: PHP extension in upload directory generates Wazuh FIM alert on file creation; correlating upload HTTP request with subsequent GET request to shell path is straightforward but requires log joining.
scenarios.attack_auth_rationale: Attacker requires a valid low-privilege user account to reach the upload endpoint.

event_log.detail: Wazuh rule 550 level 9 fired (FIM): new PHP file created in /uploads/ directory — shell.jpg (MIME: application/x-php). Concurrent access log shows POST /upload followed by GET /uploads/shell.jpg returning 200.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 500s, within 750s medium threshold. FIM event correlated with upload access log; PHP execution confirmed from HTTP response body containing 'uid=www-data'.
Analyst-2 confirmed active web shell: GET /uploads/shell.jpg?cmd=id returned server user. File content inspection confirmed PHP exec() wrapper.
Analyst-3 removed shell.jpg from uploads directory; web server reconfigured to deny PHP execution in /uploads/ (AddHandler removed); uploading user account suspended.

GET /uploads/shell.jpg returns 403. PHP execution disabled in uploads directory. Upload endpoint now enforces MIME content inspection in addition to extension check. TTC_actual=3800s < TTC_expected=4320s.

scenarios.label: Unauthenticated Web Shell via Double Extension — Containment Failure
scenarios.description: Unauthenticated attacker uploads a web shell using a null-byte double extension (shell.php%00.jpg) to bypass extension validation. The web shell executes and establishes a reverse shell. MTTA is breached and no containment occurs before TTC_max.

component_rationale.AC_H: Null-byte injection technique requires specific server-side language behaviour (PHP < 5.3.4)
component_rationale.PR_N: No authentication required; upload endpoint publicly accessible
component_rationale.S_U: Web shell execution scope confined to web-server process
component_rationale.C_H: Full filesystem read accessible to web process
component_rationale.I_H: Arbitrary write and code execution; reverse shell established
component_rationale.A_H: Attacker can disrupt service by overwriting critical web application files

scenarios.detection_difficulty_rationale: Null-byte encoding in filename is not visible in standard access log display; FIM alert fires on file creation but the filename appears as a JPEG; only raw byte inspection reveals the embedded PHP extension.

event_log.detail: Wazuh rule 551 level 9 fired (FIM): new file created in /uploads/ — file name contains null byte preceding .jpg extension; MIME type detected as application/x-php by server content inspection.

Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 1050s, exceeds 900s high threshold. FUP-R01 triggered. Analyst reviewed FIM alert but filename appeared as shell.jpg in log display; PHP content missed at first inspection.

Analyst-2 confirmed PHP web shell via raw file inspection: null byte in filename truncated display; file executed by PHP interpreter. Reverse shell connection to attacker C2 already established before investigation began.

FUP-R03.note: No containment_initiated event ever recorded; reverse shell active before containment attempted; TTC_max=10260s passed with no response

FUP-R04.note: containment_succeeded never recorded; escalation sent to wrong response team

FUP-R05.note: No alert_denied event; incident confirmed but containment never executed

test_assertion.notes: No containment → TTC contribution=0. Penalties: FUP-R01(−15)+FUP-R03(−25)+FUP-R04(−30)=−70. Final=100−70=30. R02 suppressed because incident_confirmed is present.

scenarios.label: Authenticated Double-Extension Bypass — Very-High Difficulty, Good Response
scenarios.description: Authenticated low-privilege user uploads a web shell using a .php.jpg double extension to bypass MIME validation without null bytes. High attack complexity (WAF partial block). Scope unchanged, limited integrity impact. Despite very-high detection difficulty the SOC contains within the expected window.

component_rationale.AC_H: Double-extension technique requires knowing web server AddHandler configuration
component_rationale.PR_L: Low-privilege authenticated user required to reach upload endpoint
component_rationale.S_U: Web shell execution confined to web-server process
component_rationale.C_H: Full filesystem read accessible to web process account
component_rationale.I_L: Limited write: attacker can create files in upload directory only

scenarios.detection_difficulty_rationale: Double extension .php.jpg does not trigger standard FIM name-pattern alerts; detection requires content-inspection of all uploaded files, which is computationally expensive and not always enabled by default.
scenarios.attack_auth_rationale: Low-privilege user session required for the upload endpoint.

event_log.detail: Wazuh rule 552 level 6 fired: content-inspection module flagged PHP tags inside uploaded file shell.php.jpg in /uploads/; file served by Apache due to AddHandler php5-script in .htaccess.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 1100s, within 1200s very-high threshold. Content-inspection log confirmed PHP inside shell.php.jpg. Accessed GET /uploads/shell.php.jpg — returned PHP execution output.
Analyst-2 confirmed web shell active; executed 'ls /var/www/html' via shell. Shell.php.jpg identified as persistent webshell; attacker has read access to application source code and config.
Analyst-3 removed shell.php.jpg; disabled .htaccess AddHandler override; restricted uploads to image/jpeg and image/png MIME only; deploying file content-inspection as mandatory upload gate.
Re-upload of test PHP file rejected with 415 Unsupported Media Type. Web shell removed and no longer accessible. TTC_actual=13000s < TTC_expected=14760s.
